data.title: Splash Beverage Group Secures $2 Million Financing
data.description:

data.title: Shoe Carnival Appoints New CFO Amid Expansion Plans
data.description:
Shoe Carnival ( (SCVL) ) has issued an announcement.

Shoe Carnival announced the appointment of W. Kerry Jackson as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective September 28, 2025. Jackson, who rejoined the company in June 2025 as Senior Vice President of New Business Development after retiring in May 2023, previously served as the company’s CFO for 27 years. This transition is part of Shoe Carnival’s strategic plan to expand its store count and strengthen its market position, as evidenced by the recent grand opening of its 100th Shoe Station store, with plans to exceed 215 stores by July 2026. Patrick C. Edwards, the current CFO, will transition to the role of Senior Vice President, Special Projects, and Treasurer.

The most recent analyst rating on (SCVL) stock is a Buy with a $29.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Shoe Carnival stock, see the SCVL Stock Forecast page.

To see Spark’s full report on SCVL stock, click here.

More about Shoe Carnival

Shoe Carnival, Inc. is a leading retailer of footwear and accessories for the family, offering a broad assortment of dress, casual, and athletic footwear for men, women, and children, with an emphasis on national name brands. As of September 25, 2025, the company operated 428 stores in 35 states and Puerto Rico under its Shoe Carnival, Shoe Station, and Rogan’s store fronts, and offers online shopping through its websites. The company is headquartered in Fort Mill, SC, with distribution and support operations in Evansville, IN, and trades on Nasdaq under the symbol SCVL.

Seanergy Maritime Holdings Corp. announced its 2025 Annual Meeting of Shareholders scheduled for October 30, 2025, in Glyfada, Greece. The company reported strong financial performance in 2024 with record net revenues and profitability, despite facing a challenging environment characterized by inflation and geopolitical uncertainty. In the first half of 2025, Seanergy navigated a seasonal slowdown but returned to profitability in Q2. The company maintained its shareholder-friendly dividend policy and outperformed the Baltic Capesize Index, highlighting its commitment to operational excellence and sustainable growth. Shareholders will vote on the re-election of directors and the ratification of independent auditors at the upcoming meeting.

The most recent analyst rating on (SHIP) stock is a Buy with a $9.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Seanergy Maritime stock, see the SHIP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SHIP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SHIP is a Outperform.

Seanergy Maritime’s strong financial performance and attractive valuation are key strengths, supported by positive market trends. However, technical indicators suggest caution due to potential overbought conditions, and operational challenges noted in the earnings call temper the outlook.

To see Spark’s full report on SHIP stock, click here.

More about Seanergy Maritime

Seanergy Maritime Holdings Corp. is an international shipping company specializing in the seaborne transportation of dry bulk commodities, including iron ore, bauxite, and coal. The company operates a fleet of twenty vessels, primarily consisting of Capesize and Newcastlemax vessels, and is a prominent player in the U.S. capital markets as a pure-play Capesize shipping company.

data.title: BBVA Argentina Faces Class Action Over Credit Card Interest Rates
data.description:
Banco BBVA Argentina ( (BBAR) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Banco BBVA Argentina S.A. has been notified of a class action lawsuit filed by the Protegiendo al Consumidor association. The lawsuit challenges the constitutionality of a decree that eliminated the limit on punitive interest, seeking reimbursement for excess amounts collected from credit card users in Buenos Aires. While the financial impact of the lawsuit is currently indeterminate, the bank believes that even an adverse ruling would not significantly affect its operations.

The company intends to rectify the deficiency within this period to avoid potential delisting.
